output:
CVE-2015-10023 identifies a critical SQL injection vulnerability within the Fumon trello-octometric project. Specifically, this flaw is located in the function 'main' of the file 'metrics-ui/server/srv.go' where manipulation of the 'num' argument can lead to unintended SQL command execution. This vulnerability is classified under CWE-89, which involves improper neutralization of special elements in SQL commands. 

The vulnerability is associated with MITRE ATT&CK technique T1190, indicating exploitation paths through external interfaces. Affected software versions are those of trello-octometric prior to January 25, 2015. Immediate application of the provided patch, identified as a1f1754933fbf21e2221fbc671c81a47de6a04ef, is recommended to mitigate this issue.

The critical severity of this vulnerability suggests severe potential impacts, including unauthorized data access or loss. Implementing mitigation strategies such as those recommended (M1048, M1037, M1030, M1016, M1026, M1050, M1035, M1051) can aide in minimizing risk.
